message: Morning team! Feeling pumped for the big ?Hipster Haul? event this week?
Absolutely! Can't wait to see all our hard work pay off.
Agreed, it's going to be a game-changer for us
Customer support team standing by to handle any queries. Let's do this!
Team, the excitement is palpable! I have full confidence in each of you to make this event a roaring success.
Let's showcase our best and leave our customers thrilled with what we have to offer. Together, we'll make it happen!

:moneybag: :moneybag: :moneybag:

Welcome to Details Matter, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

How about a tropical paradise with no Wi-Fi? :desert_island:
Hey folks, hate to rain on the parade, but I've got some new reports coming in about customers encountering issues with the products on the website.
Yeah, Bob, anything specific you're noticing? Error messages, affected payment methods?
Customers are not able to place orders
Alright team, let's get to work. Tinus, you can start planning that holiday once we've got this sorted!.
Deal! But first, let's get to the bottom of this issue
@bob Are the reports coming in from any particular region?
Hey team, just dropping in to emphasise the urgency of resolving this issue swiftly. We've got a major event on the horizon - the launch of our exclusive "Hipster Haul Weekend" next week.
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
It's poised to be our biggest sale yet, and any delays caused by this payment glitch could seriously impact our preparations.
Let's prioritise frequent updates and keep the communication channels wide open.
We have a lot of recent changes made to the platform so im going to compile a list with the changes and the descriptions.
Will send it over soon
@shay Thanks, can you get that over in the next two minutes please?
I'll take a look now

from what i can tell most of the reports seems to be coming from the two regions

Anyone else?

HTTP Status: 500 Internal Server Error

:star: Reviewed changes

I'll take a look now

The change I made impacts all currencies, so if there was an error it wouldn't be specific
I'll have to remind myself of the deployment once sec
SEV-3 We have identified the issue only looks to impact customers attempting to purchase in JPY and TRY at this time and are looking into the cause
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
Let me know in the next few minutes please
Oh yes I remember now, I was just optimizing the flow with a specific focus on bug fixes for the UK, so nothing related to the impacted currencies
@bob Can you let impacted customers know that they can try and complet orders using USD or GBP at this time

Change CHG89873 has been sucessfully rolledback
Good spot Daniel @hamed can you advise on CHG89692? This was a few hours ago but it did update the API key. could this be related? Do let me know asap
Hopefully that solved the issue
I made the most recent change to the currency service which is basically a security patch, I don't see how it would be causing this issue.
ill check the platform just incase it hasn't made anything worse
Can we confirm if everythings back working?
No change on the site yet. We'll need to continue to investigate
What's the impact on revenue?
@tanya Is Hamed in this week? I can see they made a change earlier today to the CurrencyService API, can we confirm that key is correct?
About 9% of revenue comes through from the impacted regions combined on a monthly basis
He is online
Great. Can you and @hamed look into CHG89692 in the next few minutes please and let me know if that Key is okay?
He might be supporting another call, give him another nudge though
Apologies I'm here now
I'm not questioning your judgement but why do you think this issue is related to the change I made?
Sev-2 Whilst an attempted solution has been made, the issue continues and revenue remains impacted in these regions. Customers can look to purchase in GBP or USD whilst we investigate the issue

We're seeing an unauthorized error in the logs when attempting to view the products whilst set to JPY to TRY at this time. It might not be the key itself but it does suggest the conversion here may be causing an issue
:star: Form a working theory
Have we definitely ruled out other options?
I can't replicate the issue on a specific product either at this time, unless someone has been able to

For me, I get issues when switching to the yen and lira
Which aligns with Bob's report
Same. Happy to hear any ideas you may have Hamed
My change passed all the regression test, I just wanted to make sure all other avenues had been explored before escalating to me
Previous changes to the CurrencyService have been rolled back but the issue continues, hence the escalation for review
:star: Escalating when required
I just want to confirm that we've definitely tracked the issue to being related to the currency service and perhaps not payments like Tanya's change
It could be the payments service, but the products page as a whole is impacted, not just attempting to purchase products. Would that be impacted to that level?
Happy to get Tanya to rolle that back whilst we investigate other avenues
It's up to you what direction we go in
@tanya Are you able to start rolling back CHG89675 whilst Hamed looks into the other changes? Just to rule anything out that's been done since the reports began
:star: Form an effective investigation team
@bob Did the reports start 3 hours ago or longer?
Due to the bug fixes it's unwise to rollback my change, also didn't Daniel find an error pointing to this being currency service related
The reports have only started coming in within the last 10 minutes
Okay. I still feel it may be the currency service at this time causing the issue, or a missed dependency not having been restarted
Apologies guys, it's oversight on my end, it looks like the API keys I implemented have been misconfigured
Least you spotted it
Can we get that updated ASAP please @hamed?
Get Tanya to help if needed
we're gonna need a plan in order to do this Fix for the API keys
Update: We believe we have identified the cause of the issue and are implementing a fix now
We need to contact the third-party currency exchange rate service provider to request a new API key.
Also will need to secure the secrets manager to store the new API key, just to make sure its not hard-coded into source code and prevent unauthorised access.
someone needs to update the configuration of the currency service to use the new API key for accessing the third-party service
What do you think @PLAYER?
Thanks for the info. Let's split into teams here

Fix is now Implemented

What a ride! Massive thanks to each of you for your quick thinking and teamwork.
Absolutely, Shay! Getting more confident with any incidents that come our way. Good Job everyone!!
@hamed thanks for ensuring we double check things here
Phew, what a rollercoaster! Glad we managed to fix it before things got out of hand.
Agreed
Good job everyone, but im confused how did those currencies fail recently if the change was made hours ago?
Good question @Tinus. The currency_service was using 'cache data' instead of getting the data from 3rd party service as i messed up the configuration of the APIs.
The cache data expires every 12 hours as well which must have been around the time Bob was getting the new reports.
Understood, thanks for clearing that up for me @Hamed.
Anyways with the fix in place, we've ensured that the system operates smoothly, and we're now ready once again for the big event next week.
Fantastic work, everyone! Your quick and effective response has saved the day. Now, let's channel this momentum into making our Hipster Haul Weekend a smashing success.
Update: The fix has been implemented and we are no longer seeing an issue
